raw_pick_text: Karl-Anthony Towns over 18.5 Points Scored -116
raw_block: Points Scored Karl-Anthony Towns o18.5 Points Scored (-116) Best Odds o19.5 -112 These are quick bet odds Pick made: 4 hours ago Jon Metler Betting Analyst Analysis o20.5 -102 These are quick bet odds o19.5 -112 These are quick bet odds o19.5 -120 These are quick bet odds o19.5 -140 These are quick bet odds o19.5 -122 These are quick bet odds Karl-Anthony Towns has been an “under” machine on his points prop lately, but much of that is a byproduct of game script. The New York Knicks have won four of their last seven games by 26 points or more, limiting Towns’ minutes and shot volume. Because of those recent unders, the market has pushed KAT’s points total down to 18.5, creating value in this spot. Towns draws a strong matchup against Nikola Jokić, who lacks foot speed and often compensates by roaming the paint and using his size to contest drives. That approach won’t work here, as Towns will pull Jokić out to the perimeter and force him to defend in space. If Aaron Gordon were available, Denver could adjust defensively, but with him sidelined, those options are limited. I see a significant edge at 18.5, which I price much closer to -220.

raw_block: Points Scored De'Aaron Fox o16.5 Points Scored (-112) Best Odds o16.5 -114 These are quick bet odds Pick made: 2 hours ago Jon Metler Betting Analyst Analysis o17.5 -101 These are quick bet odds o16.5 -115 These are quick bet odds o16.5 -114 These are quick bet odds o16.5 -120 These are quick bet odds o16.5 -120 These are quick bet odds De’Aaron Fox’s points prop is trading at -112 for over 16.5 points, and that’s a number we can attack, as I believe it should be priced much closer to -165. On the surface, the Thunder owning the best defense in the NBA doesn’t scream “over,” but Fox has consistently shown all season that he’s a tough matchup for the way Oklahoma City likes to defend. Fox is one of the fastest players in the league and can consistently push the pace in transition before the Thunder are able to get set defensively. That speed becomes even more problematic for a Thunder team playing on the second night of a back-to-back. On top of that, Fox can leverage the gravity of Victor Wembanyama as a lob threat, allowing him to generate clean mid-range jumpers and floaters against Isaiah Hartenstein and Chet Holmgren.

With Evan Mobley out and Darius Garland shipped to the Clippers, Mitchell’s usage will spike—especially late in the game—with the Cavaliers favored by just 2.5 points. While James Harden technically only needs to change locker rooms, he has been away from the team amid his trade request, so I don’t expect him to suit up on Wednesday. Kris Dunn is a strong point-of-attack defender, but the sheer volume Mitchell should see makes that matchup less concerning. With the ball in his hands all night, Mitchell is well-positioned to clear this 29.5-point total.
